title: buildah bud broken?
body:
Hey,
as I was trying to learn about building my own containers, I stumbled upon this issue,while trying to add a chrootdir or the ubuntu rootfs tarball (mind you alpine rootfs works 100%):
```
$ buildah bud -t ubuntu
STEP 1: FROM scratch
STEP 2: ADD focal-server-cloudimg-amd64-root.tar.xz /
error building at STEP "ADD focal-server-cloudimg-amd64-root.tar.xz /": error adding sources [/home/user/Containers/ubuntu/focal-server-cloudimg-amd64-root.tar.xz]: 3 errors occurred:
        * error reading "/home/user/Containers/ubuntu/focal-server-cloudimg-amd64-root.tar.xz": error during bulk transfer for copier.request{Request:"GET", Root:"/", preservedRoot:"/home/user/Containers/ubuntu", rootPrefix:"/home/user/Containers/ubuntu", Directory:"/", preservedDirectory:"/home/user/Containers/ubuntu", Globs:[]string{"/focal-server-cloudimg-amd64-root.tar.xz"}, preservedGlobs:[]string{"/home/user/Containers/ubuntu/focal-server-cloudimg-amd64-root.tar.xz"}, StatOptions:copier.StatOptions{CheckForArchives:true, Excludes:[]string(nil)}, GetOptions:copier.GetOptions{UIDMap:[]idtools.IDMap(nil), GIDMap:[]idtools.IDMap(nil), Excludes:[]string{""}, ExpandArchives:true, ChownDirs:(*idtools.IDPair)(0xc000289920), ChmodDirs:(*os.FileMode)(nil), ChownFiles:(*idtools.IDPair)(0xc000289930), ChmodFiles:(*os.FileMode)(nil), StripSetuidBit:true, StripSetgidBit:true, StripStickyBit:false, StripXattrs:false, KeepDirectoryNames:false}, PutOptions:copier.PutOptions{UIDMap:[]idtools.IDMap(nil), GIDMap:[]idtools.IDMap(nil), DefaultDirOwner:(*idtools.IDPair)(nil), DefaultDirMode:(*os.FileMode)(nil), ChownDirs:(*idtools.IDPair)(nil), ChmodDirs:(*os.FileMode)(nil), ChownFiles:(*idtools.IDPair)(nil), ChmodFiles:(*os.FileMode)(nil), StripXattrs:false, IgnoreXattrErrors:false}, MkdirOptions:copier.MkdirOptions{UIDMap:[]idtools.IDMap(nil), GIDMap:[]idtools.IDMap(nil), ChownNew:(*idtools.IDPair)(nil), ChmodNew:(*os.FileMode)(nil)}}: copier: get: "/focal-server-cloudimg-amd64-root.tar.xz": error writing tar header from "/focal-server-cloudimg-amd64-root.tar.xz" to pipe: write bulk-writer: broken pipe
        * error closing "/home/user/Containers/ubuntu/focal-server-cloudimg-amd64-root.tar.xz": error reading tar archive: error filtering tar header for "etc/NetworkManager/dispatcher.d/hook-network-manager": io: read/write on closed pipe
        * error storing "/home/user/Containers/ubuntu/focal-server-cloudimg-amd64-root.tar.xz": error during bulk transfer for copier.request{Request:"PUT", Root:"/", preservedRoot:"/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/6213053cf7a25781db1e49d084f89ad8c12c30b1794ce48910036f786f1c0b11/merged", rootPrefix:"/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/6213053cf7a25781db1e49d084f89ad8c12c30b1794ce48910036f786f1c0b11/merged", Directory:"/", preservedDirectory:"/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/6213053cf7a25781db1e49d084f89ad8c12c30b1794ce48910036f786f1c0b11/merged", Globs:[]string{}, preservedGlobs:[]string{}, StatOptions:copier.StatOptions{CheckForArchives:false, Excludes:[]string(nil)}, GetOptions:copier.GetOptions{UIDMap:[]idtools.IDMap(nil), GIDMap:[]idtools.IDMap(nil), Excludes:[]string(nil), ExpandArchives:false, ChownDirs:(*idtools.IDPair)(nil), ChmodDirs:(*os.FileMode)(nil), ChownFiles:(*idtools.IDPair)(nil), ChmodFiles:(*os.FileMode)(nil), StripSetuidBit:false, StripSetgidBit:false, StripStickyBit:false, StripXattrs:false, KeepDirectoryNames:false}, PutOptions:copier.PutOptions{UIDMap:[]idtools.IDMap{}, GIDMap:[]idtools.IDMap{}, DefaultDirOwner:(*idtools.IDPair)(0xc0004e71c0), DefaultDirMode:(*os.FileMode)(nil), ChownDirs:(*idtools.IDPair)(nil), ChmodDirs:(*os.FileMode)(nil), ChownFiles:(*idtools.IDPair)(nil), ChmodFiles:(*os.FileMode)(nil), StripXattrs:false, IgnoreXattrErrors:false}, MkdirOptions:copier.MkdirOptions{UIDMap:[]idtools.IDMap(nil), GIDMap:[]idtools.IDMap(nil), ChownNew:(*idtools.IDPair)(nil), ChmodNew:(*os.FileMode)(nil)}}: copier: put: error creating "/dev/console": operation not permitted


ERRO exit status 125
```

Further digging in, I found the last good version (I can test easily) is 1.15.2 which resides in ubuntu 20.10 (or 21.04) but my main system on debian bullseye with 1.16.6 doesn't. Adding the kubic repo to ubuntu verifies that it breaks :/. 

Honestly I can't really make anything out of this error message. would be nice to get some help.

//edit: just to add, using sudo works perfectly fine.

comments.body: My first thought would be running out of disk space in your home dir, but the `error creating "/dev/console": operation not permitted` might otherwise be an indicator....
Rootless users are not allowed to create devices.  So the problem is creating a device.

so how come, ubuntu with 1.15.2 and opensuse leap with 1.14.6 works perfectly fine? can I see that as a bug with those packaged (their own patches?) software?

//edit: looked into the changelog, and didn't see anything particular about this (if there was a change)
It could be older versions were ignored?

could you show the output for `tar tvf focal-server-cloudimg-amd64-root.tar.xz`?

My guess is that a device is part of the tarball itself and buildah fails to create it.

I am not sure we should skip devices present in the tarball, if that is what the user asked to add.

wouldn't it then not also not work on 1.15.2 and lower?

here is the gist of it (so it doesn't clutter too much) https://gist.github.com/mailing-lists/4857e9e3c17612828d9ef5c02458a2ad

@nalind WDYT?  DId we used to ignore devices in tar balls?

@mailing-lists could this be a case where you tried it as root and it worked and now you are running rootless and it is failing?
I always tried it with rootless. that's why I stumbled upon the bug. If I build it as root, it works fine.

We used to use the containers/storage `archive` package to extract archives that were being ADDed, and it ignored such entries if it was told that it was in a user namespace, but we switched to using the `copier` package to do that work in 1.16, and it doesn't currently allow for ignoring this error.
Can you add a fix into the new code to allow ignoring when running in rootless mode.
